title: Half Adder
prompt: Write a Verilog module for a half adder using dataflow (assign) modeling.
constraints: Inputs: a, b. Outputs: sum, carry. Use assign statements only.
expected_output: sum = a XOR b, carry = a AND b

title: Full Adder
prompt: Implement a full adder with carry-in using behavioral (always) modeling.
constraints: Inputs: a, b, cin. Outputs: sum, cout (reg type).
expected_output: sum = a XOR b XOR cin, cout = majority(a,b,cin)

title: D Flip-Flop with Async Reset
prompt: Implement a D flip-flop with active-low asynchronous reset.
constraints: Inputs: clk, rst_n, d. Output: q (reg). Reset sets q to 0.
expected_output: q captures d on posedge clk; rst_n=0 forces q=0 immediately

title: 4-to-1 Multiplexer
prompt: Build a 4-to-1 MUX using a case statement in an always block.

title: 8-bit Synchronous Counter with Enable and Reset
prompt: Write an 8-bit up-counter with synchronous active-high reset and count enable.
constraints: Inputs: clk, rst (sync), en. Output: count[7:0] (reg).
expected_output: count increments on each posedge when en=1; rst synchronously clears to 0

title: Parameterized Shift Register
prompt: Implement a shift register with configurable WIDTH parameter.
constraints: Parameter: WIDTH=8. Inputs: clk, rst_n, d (serial in). Output: q[WIDTH-1:0].
expected_output: Each clock, data shifts left (q[0] gets d; q[WIDTH-1] is oldest)

title: Moore FSM: Sequence Detector (1011)
prompt: Design a Moore FSM that outputs 1 when the input sequence '1011' is detected (overlapping).
constraints: Inputs: clk, rst_n, in. Output: out (reg). States: S0, S1, S2, S3, S4.
expected_output: out=1 only one cycle after detecting ...1011; sequence may overlap

title: Synchronous FIFO
prompt: Write a synchronous FIFO with DEPTH=8 and DATA_WIDTH=8. Include full and empty flags.
constraints: Inputs: clk, rst_n, wr_en, rd_en, din. Outputs: dout, full, empty.
expected_output: Write when wr_en and not full; read when rd_en and not empty; full/empty reflect state

title: Parameterized Up-Counter (always_ff)
prompt: Create a WIDTH-parameterized up-counter with synchronous clear using always_ff.
constraints: Use logic type and always_ff. Inputs: clk, rst_n (async), clr (sync). Output: cnt[WIDTH-1:0].
expected_output: cnt increments each clock; rst_n=0 async resets; clr=1 sync clears

title: 4-to-2 Priority Encoder (always_comb)
prompt: Implement a 4-to-2 priority encoder with valid flag using always_comb and unique casez.
constraints: Input: req[3:0]. Outputs: enc[1:0], valid. Bit 3 has highest priority.
expected_output: enc outputs highest priority asserted bit index; valid=0 when req=0

title: Round-Robin Arbiter
prompt: Design a 4-input round-robin arbiter. Grant rotates among requestors to ensure fairness.
constraints: Inputs: clk, rst_n, req[3:0]. Output: gnt[3:0] (one-hot). Use always_ff and always_comb.
expected_output: Only one grant bit set per cycle; grant rotates among requestors in order

title: UVM Transaction (uvm_sequence_item)
prompt: Create a UVM transaction class for a simple memory bus: address, data, write flag. Include field macros and a constraint.
constraints: Extend uvm_sequence_item. Use rand fields. Constraint: addr aligned to 4 bytes. Include `uvm_object_utils_begin macro block.
expected_output: Transaction class with rand addr[31:0], data[31:0], write bit; addr%4==0 constraint

title: UVM Sequence: Directed Write/Read
prompt: Write a UVM sequence that sends 5 write transactions followed by 5 read transactions to the same addresses.
constraints: Extend uvm_sequence #(mem_txn). Use start_item / finish_item pattern. Override body() task.
expected_output: Sequence sends 5 writes (write=1) then 5 reads (write=0) to addresses 0,4,8,12,16

title: UVM Driver: Pin-Level APB Driver
prompt: Implement a UVM driver for APB protocol that drives address, write data, and control signals from a mem_txn transaction.
constraints: Extend uvm_driver #(mem_txn). Use a virtual apb_if. Override run_phase. Loop forever: get_next_item, drive_apb, item_done.
expected_output: Driver drives PSEL, PENABLE, PADDR, PWDATA, PWRITE; waits for PREADY
